title: Launched pangea8.com
body: Today I officially launched pangea8.com — my IELTS practice hub. It hosts mock tests for Reading, Writing, Listening, and Speaking, plus the Pangeya Essay platform. Built it for my students, but it's open to anyone preparing for IELTS. Setting up the domain through Cloudflare and connecting it to GitHub Pages took an afternoon. The hardest part was choosing the name.

title: Why Pangea?
body: Pangea was the single supercontinent before everything drifted apart. I liked the metaphor — one world, one platform. The 8 in pangea8 stands for IELTS Band 8 (the goal most of my students aim for), and also looks like the infinity symbol when laid sideways. Pangea forever, Pangea eight. Brand names should mean something to the founder, even if nobody else gets it at first.

title: Making music from Tashkent
body: Releasing UK Garage tracks from Uzbekistan feels strange sometimes. I'm probably one of the only producers in Central Asia making this sound. My album HOME is out on Spotify under Matsumi. Most of my listeners are in the UK, which makes sense — that's where the genre lives. But it also tells me something: location doesn't matter anymore. The internet erased that.
